{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_801dbf83831442719c0c1708","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Mode A shard-partitioned ownership coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode A).\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Each pod owns a subset of shards for background processing. Assignment uses\n4\t//! rendezvous hashing over the current peer set:\n5\t//!\n6\t//! ```text\n7\t//! peers      = discover_peers()           // headless-Service DNS lookup\n8\t//! owns(s, p) = p == top1_by_score(hash(s || pid) for pid in peers)\n9\t//! ```\n10\t//!\n11\t//! Applies to:\n12\t//! - Anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) — each pod fingerprints and repairs the shards it owns\n13\t//! - Settings drift check (§13.5) — each pod polls a subset of (index, node) settings-hash pairs\n14\t//! - Task registry pruner — each pod prunes tasks where it wins the rendezvous score\n15\t//! - TTL sweeper (§13.14) — each pod sweeps only its rendezvous-owned shards\n16\t//! - Canary runner (§13.18) — each canary ID is rendezvous-owned by exactly one pod\n17\t//!\n18\t//! When the peer set changes (scale event, pod restart), rendezvous redistributes\n19\t//! ownership with minimal reshuffling. No explicit handoff — the new owner runs\n20\t//! the next scheduled pass. Transient double-work during a 15-second discovery\n21\t//! window is harmless: all operations are idempotent.\n22\t\n23\tuse crate::peer_discovery::{PeerDiscovery, PeerId, PeerSet};\n24\tuse std::hash::Hasher;\n25\tuse std::sync::Arc;\n26\tuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n27\tuse tracing::{debug, warn};\n28\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n29\t\n30\t/// Error type for Mode A coordination.\n31\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, thiserror::Error)]\n32\tpub enum ModeAError {\n33\t    #[error(\"no peers discovered\")]\n34\t    NoPeers,\n35\t\n36\t    #[error(\"empty shard identifier\")]\n37\t    EmptyShardId,\n38\t\n39\t    #[error(\"empty peer identifier\")]\n40\t    EmptyPeerId,\n41\t}\n42\t\n43\t/// Result type for Mode A operations.\n44\tpub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, ModeAError>;\n45\t\n46\t/// Mode A coordinator for shard-partitioned ownership.\n47\t///\n48\t/// Uses rendezvous hashing over the current peer set to determine which pod\n49\t/// owns a given shard or task.\n50\tpub struct ModeACoordinator {\n51\t    /// Our own pod ID (from POD_NAME env var).\n52\t    pod_id: PeerId,\n53\t    /// Peer discovery service.\n54\t    peer_discovery: Arc<PeerDiscovery>,\n55\t    /// Cached peer set (refreshed periodically).\n56\t    cached_peer_set: Arc<RwLock<PeerSet>>,\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\timpl ModeACoordinator {\n60\t    /// Create a new Mode A coordinator.\n61\t    ///\n62\t    /// # Arguments\n63\t    ///\n64\t    /// * `pod_id` - Our pod ID (from `POD_NAME` env var)\n65\t    /// * `peer_discovery` - Peer discovery service\n66\t    pub fn new(pod_id: PeerId, peer_discovery: Arc<PeerDiscovery>) -> Self {\n67\t        let peer_set = PeerSet::new(vec![pod_id.clone()]);\n68\t        Self {\n69\t            pod_id,\n70\t            peer_discovery,\n71\t            cached_peer_set: Arc::new(RwLock::new(peer_set)),\n72\t        }\n73\t    }\n74\t\n75\t    /// Refresh the peer set from DNS SRV records.\n76\t    ///\n77\t    /// Should be called periodically (e.g., every 15s per plan §14.5).\n78\t    pub async fn refresh_peers(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n79\t        let new_peer_set = self.peer_discovery.refresh().await.map_err(|e| {\n80\t            warn!(\"peer discovery failed: {}\", e);\n81\t            ModeAError::NoPeers\n82\t        })?;\n83\t\n84\t        let peer_count = new_peer_set.peers.len();\n85\t        if peer_count == 0 {\n86\t            warn!(\"peer discovery returned empty peer set\");\n87\t            return Err(ModeAError::NoPeers);\n88\t        }\n89\t\n90\t        // Update cached peer set\n91\t        let mut cached = self.cached_peer_set.write().await;\n92\t        *cached = new_peer_set;\n93\t\n94\t        debug!(\n95\t            pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n96\t            peer_count,\n97\t            \"refreshed Mode A peer set\"\n98\t        );\n99\t\n100\t        Ok(peer_count)\n101\t    }\n102\t\n103\t    /// Get the current peer set.\n104\t    pub async fn peer_set(&self) -> PeerSet {\n105\t        self.cached_peer_set.read().await.clone()\n106\t    }\n107\t\n108\t    /// Compute the rendezvous score for a shard-peer pair.\n109\t    ///\n110\t    /// Higher score = higher ownership priority.\n111\t    /// Uses xxhash (twox-hash) for consistency with router.\n112\t    fn rendezvous_score(shard_id: &str, peer_id: &str) -> u64 {\n113\t        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n114\t        hasher.write(shard_id.as_bytes());\n115\t        hasher.write(b\"||\");\n116\t        hasher.write(peer_id.as_bytes());\n117\t        hasher.finish()\n118\t    }\n119\t\n120\t    /// Find the peer that owns a given shard via rendezvous hashing.\n121\t    ///\n122\t    /// Returns the peer ID with the highest rendezvous score for the shard.\n123\t    pub async fn owner_for_shard(&self, shard_id: &str) -> Result<PeerId> {\n124\t        if shard_id.is_empty() {\n125\t            return Err(ModeAError::EmptyShardId);\n126\t        }\n127\t\n128\t        let peer_set = self.peer_set().await;\n129\t\n130\t        if peer_set.peers.is_empty() {\n131\t            return Err(ModeAError::NoPeers);\n132\t        }\n133\t\n134\t        let mut best_peer = None;\n135\t        let mut best_score = 0u64;\n136\t\n137\t        for peer in &peer_set.peers {\n138\t            let score = Self::rendezvous_score(shard_id, peer);\n139\t            if score > best_score {\n140\t                best_score = score;\n141\t                best_peer = Some(peer.clone());\n142\t            }\n143\t        }\n144\t\n145\t        best_peer.ok_or(ModeAError::NoPeers)\n146\t    }\n147\t\n148\t    /// Check if this pod owns a given shard.\n149\t    ///\n150\t    /// Returns true if this pod has the highest rendezvous score for the shard.\n151\t    pub async fn owns_shard(&self, shard_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n152\t        if shard_id.is_empty() {\n153\t            return Err(ModeAError::EmptyShardId);\n154\t        }\n155\t\n156\t        let owner = self.owner_for_shard(shard_id).await?;\n157\t        Ok(owner == self.pod_id)\n158\t    }\n159\t\n160\t    /// Check if this pod owns a task (by miroir_id).\n161\t    ///\n162\t    /// Uses the same rendezvous hashing as shard ownership.\n163\t    /// Task registry pruner uses this to partition task cleanup.\n164\t    pub async fn owns_task(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n165\t        if miroir_id.is_empty() {\n166\t            return Err(ModeAError::EmptyShardId);\n167\t        }\n168\t\n169\t        let peer_set = self.peer_set().await;\n170\t\n171\t        if peer_set.peers.is_empty() {\n172\t            return Err(ModeAError::NoPeers);\n173\t        }\n174\t\n175\t        let mut best_score = 0u64;\n176\t        let mut is_owner = false;\n177\t\n178\t        for peer in &peer_set.peers {\n179\t            let score = Self::rendezvous_score(miroir_id, peer);\n180\t            if score > best_score {\n181\t                best_score = score;\n182\t                is_owner = peer == &self.pod_id;\n183\t            }\n184\t        }\n185\t\n186\t        Ok(is_owner)\n187\t    }\n188\t\n189\t    /// Synchronous version of `owns_task` for use with sync callbacks.\n190\t    ///\n191\t    /// Uses `try_read` on the cached peer set to avoid blocking.\n192\t    /// Returns `true` if ownership can be determined and this pod owns the task,\n193\t    /// `false` if ownership can be determined and this pod doesn't own it,\n194\t    /// or `false` if the peer set lock is contended (safe default: skip).\n195\t    ///\n196\t    /// This is designed for use with the task pruner's `mode_a_owner_fn` callback.\n197\t    pub fn owns_task_sync(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> bool {\n198\t        if miroir_id.is_empty() {\n199\t            return false;\n200\t        }\n201\t\n202\t        // Try to read the cached peer set without blocking\n203\t        let peer_set = match self.cached_peer_set.try_read() {\n204\t            Ok(guard) => guard.clone(),\n205\t            Err(_) => {\n206\t                // Lock is contended, return false (safe default: skip this task)\n207\t                // Another pod will handle it\n208\t                return false;\n209\t            }\n210\t        };\n211\t\n212\t        if peer_set.peers.is_empty() {\n213\t            return false;\n214\t        }\n215\t\n216\t        let mut best_score = 0u64;\n217\t        let mut is_owner = false;\n218\t\n219\t        for peer in &peer_set.peers {\n220\t            let score = Self::rendezvous_score(miroir_id, peer);\n221\t            if score > best_score {\n222\t                best_score = score;\n223\t                is_owner = peer == &self.pod_id;\n224\t            }\n225\t        }\n226\t\n227\t        is_owner\n228\t    }\n229\t\n230\t    /// Check if this pod owns a canary (by canary ID).\n231\t    ///\n232\t    /// Canary runner uses this to partition canary execution.\n233\t    pub async fn owns_canary(&self, canary_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n234\t        self.owns_task(canary_id).await\n235\t    }\n236\t\n237\t    /// Check if this pod owns an (index, node) pair for settings drift checking.\n238\t    ///\n239\t    /// Combines index and node into a single key for rendezvous hashing.\n240\t    pub async fn owns_settings_check(&self, index_uid: &str, node_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n241\t        let key = format!(\"{index_uid}:{node_id}\");\n242\t        self.owns_task(&key).await\n243\t    }\n244\t\n245\t    /// Get the list of shards owned by this pod.\n246\t    ///\n247\t    /// Computes ownership for all shards and returns the ones this pod owns.\n248\t    pub async fn owned_shards(&self, all_shards: &[u32]) -> Result<Vec<u32>> {\n249\t        let peer_set = self.peer_set().await;\n250\t\n251\t        if peer_set.peers.is_empty() {\n252\t            return Err(ModeAError::NoPeers);\n253\t        }\n254\t\n255\t        let mut owned = Vec::new();\n256\t\n257\t        for &shard_id in all_shards {\n258\t            let shard_str = shard_id.to_string();\n259\t            if self.owns_shard(&shard_str).await? {\n260\t                owned.push(shard_id);\n261\t            }\n262\t        }\n263\t\n264\t        Ok(owned)\n265\t    }\n266\t\n267\t    /// Get the fraction of shards owned by this pod.\n268\t    ///\n269\t    /// Returns a value between 0.0 and 1.0.\n270\t    pub async fn ownership_fraction(&self, all_shards: &[u32]) -> Result<f64> {\n271\t        let owned = self.owned_shards(all_shards).await?;\n272\t        let total = all_shards.len() as f64;\n273\t        let owned_count = owned.len() as f64;\n274\t\n275\t        if total > 0.0 {\n276\t            Ok(owned_count / total)\n277\t        } else {\n278\t            Ok(0.0)\n279\t        }\n280\t    }\n281\t\n282\t    /// Get the current peer count.\n283\t    pub async fn peer_count(&self) -> usize {\n284\t        self.peer_set().await.peers.len()\n285\t    }\n286\t\n287\t    /// Check if we are the only peer (single-pod deployment).\n288\t    pub async fn is_single_pod(&self) -> bool {\n289\t        self.peer_count().await <= 1\n290\t    }\n291\t\n292\t    /// Get our pod ID.\n293\t    pub fn pod_id(&self) -> &str {\n294\t        &self.pod_id\n295\t    }\n296\t\n297\t    /// Set the peer set directly (test-only).\n298\t    ///\n299\t    /// This method is only intended for use in tests to simulate different\n300\t    /// peer configurations without going through peer discovery. It is reachable\n301\t    /// from in-crate unit tests (`cfg(test)`) and from integration tests / other\n302\t    /// crates that enable the `test-helpers` feature — integration tests link\n303\t    /// against the non-test build of the library, so `cfg(test)` alone would\n304\t    /// strip it.\n305\t    #[cfg(any(test, feature = \"test-helpers\"))]\n306\t    pub async fn set_peer_set_for_test(&self, peer_set: PeerSet) {\n307\t        let mut cached = self.cached_peer_set.write().await;\n308\t        *cached = peer_set;\n309\t    }\n310\t}\n311\t\n312\t#[cfg(test)]\n313\tmod tests {\n314\t    use super::*;\n315\t\n316\t    #[test]\n317\t    fn test_rendezvous_score_deterministic() {\n318\t        // Same inputs should produce same score\n319\t        let score1 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-42\", \"pod-1\");\n320\t        let score2 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-42\", \"pod-1\");\n321\t        assert_eq!(score1, score2);\n322\t    }\n323\t\n324\t    #[test]\n325\t    fn test_rendezvous_score_different_peers() {\n326\t        // Different peers should produce different scores\n327\t        let score1 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-42\", \"pod-1\");\n328\t        let score2 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-42\", \"pod-2\");\n329\t        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n330\t    }\n331\t\n332\t    #[test]\n333\t    fn test_rendezvous_score_different_shards() {\n334\t        // Different shards should produce different scores\n335\t        let score1 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-1\", \"pod-1\");\n336\t        let score2 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-2\", \"pod-1\");\n337\t        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n338\t    }\n339\t\n340\t    #[test]\n341\t    fn test_owns_shard_empty_id() {\n342\t        let coordinator = test_coordinator();\n343\t        tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap().block_on(async {\n344\t coordinator_2 = ModeACoordinator::new(\"pod-2\".to_string(), peer_discovery_2);\n437\t\n438\t        let peer_discovery_3 = Arc::new(PeerDiscovery::new(\n439\t            \"pod-3\".to_string(),\n440\t            \"default\".to_string(),\n441\t            \"miroir-headless\".to_string(),\n442\t        ));\n443\t        let coordinator_3 = ModeACoordinator::new(\"pod-3\".to_string(), peer_discovery_3);\n444\t\n445\t        // Set up a shared peer set with all 3 pods\n446\t        let peer_set = PeerSet::new(vec![\n447\t            \"pod-1\".to_string(),\n448\t            \"pod-2\".to_string(),\n449\t            \"pod-3\".to_string(),\n450\t        ]);\n451\t        *coordinator_1.cached_peer_set.write().await = peer_set.clone();\n452\t        *coordinator_2.cached_peer_set.write().await = peer_set.clone();\n453\t        *coordinator_3.cached_peer_set.write().await = peer_set;\n454\t\n455\t        // Test various items\n456\t        let test_items = vec![\"shard-0\", \"shard-1\", \"shard-42\", \"task-abc\", \"index:node-1\"];\n457\t\n458\t        for item in test_items {\n459\t            let owns_1 = coordinator_1.owns_shard(item).await.unwrap();\n460\t            let owns_2 = coordinator_2.owns_shard(item).await.unwrap();\n461\t            let owns_3 = coordinator_3.owns_shard(item).await.unwrap();\n462\t\n463\t            // Count how many pods own this item\n464\t            let owner_count = [owns_1, owns_2, owns_3].iter().filter(|&&x| x).count();\n465\t\n466\t            // Exactly one pod should own each item\n467\t            assert_eq!(\n468\t                owner_count, 1,\n469\t                \"Item '{}' should be owned by exactly one pod, but {} pods claim ownership\",\n470\t                item, owner_count\n471\t            );\n472\t\n473\t            // Verify that if a pod owns it, it knows it owns it\n474\t            if owns_1 {\n475\t                assert!(\n476\t                    !owns_2 && !owns_3,\n477\t                    \"pod-1 owns '{}', so no other pod should\",\n478\t                    item\n479\t                );\n480\t            } else if owns_2 {\n481\t                assert!(!owns_3, \"pod-2 owns '{}', so pod-3 should not\", item);\n482\t            } else {\n483\t                assert!(owns_3, \"one pod must own '{}'\", item);\n484\t            }\n485\t        }\n486\t    }\n487\t\n488\t    /// Test sync version of owns_task\n489\t    #[test]\n490\t    fn \"default\".to_string(),\n523\t            \"miroir-headless\".to_string(),\n524\t        ));\n525\t\n526\t        ModeACoordinator::new(\"test-pod\".to_string(), peer_discovery)\n527\t    }\n528\t}\n529\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"4d3bdffd-eaa4-4e94-9dc6-2a8c83a87df3","uuid":"b1da20a4-5e02-4fec-a6e6-f82f866cd8cf","timestamp":"2026-07-09T12:57:55.312Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/miroir/crates/miroir-core/src/mode_a_coordinator.rs","content":"//! Mode A shard-partitioned ownership coordinator (plan §14.5 Mode A).\n//!\n//! Each pod owns a subset of shards for background processing. Assignment uses\n//! rendezvous hashing over the current peer set:\n//!\n//! ```text\n//! peers      = discover_peers()           // headless-Service DNS lookup\n//! owns(s, p) = p == top1_by_score(hash(s || pid) for pid in peers)\n//! ```\n//!\n//! Applies to:\n//! - Anti-entropy reconciler (§13.8) — each pod fingerprints and repairs the shards it owns\n//! - Settings drift check (§13.5) — each pod polls a subset of (index, node) settings-hash pairs\n//! - Task registry pruner — each pod prunes tasks where it wins the rendezvous score\n//! - TTL sweeper (§13.14) — each pod sweeps only its rendezvous-owned shards\n//! - Canary runner (§13.18) — each canary ID is rendezvous-owned by exactly one pod\n//!\n//! When the peer set changes (scale event, pod restart), rendezvous redistributes\n//! ownership with minimal reshuffling. No explicit handoff — the new owner runs\n//! the next scheduled pass. Transient double-work during a 15-second discovery\n//! window is harmless: all operations are idempotent.\n\nuse crate::peer_discovery::{PeerDiscovery, PeerId, PeerSet};\nuse std::hash::Hasher;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, warn};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n\n/// Error type for Mode A coordination.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum ModeAError {\n    #[error(\"no peers discovered\")]\n    NoPeers,\n\n    #[error(\"empty shard identifier\")]\n    EmptyShardId,\n\n    #[error(\"empty peer identifier\")]\n    EmptyPeerId,\n}\n\n/// Result type for Mode A operations.\npub type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, ModeAError>;\n\n/// Mode A coordinator for shard-partitioned ownership.\n///\n/// Uses rendezvous hashing over the current peer set to determine which pod\n/// owns a given shard or task.\npub struct ModeACoordinator {\n    /// Our own pod ID (from POD_NAME env var).\n    pod_id: PeerId,\n    /// Peer discovery service.\n    peer_discovery: Arc<PeerDiscovery>,\n    /// Cached peer set (refreshed periodically).\n    cached_peer_set: Arc<RwLock<PeerSet>>,\n}\n\nimpl ModeACoordinator {\n    /// Create a new Mode A coordinator.\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `pod_id` - Our pod ID (from `POD_NAME` env var)\n    /// * `peer_discovery` - Peer discovery service\n    pub fn new(pod_id: PeerId, peer_discovery: Arc<PeerDiscovery>) -> Self {\n        let peer_set = PeerSet::new(vec![pod_id.clone()]);\n        Self {\n            pod_id,\n            peer_discovery,\n            cached_peer_set: Arc::new(RwLock::new(peer_set)),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Refresh the peer set from DNS SRV records.\n    ///\n    /// Should be called periodically (e.g., every 15s per plan §14.5).\n    pub async fn refresh_peers(&self) -> Result<usize> {\n        let new_peer_set = self.peer_discovery.refresh().await.map_err(|e| {\n            warn!(\"peer discovery failed: {}\", e);\n            ModeAError::NoPeers\n        })?;\n\n        let peer_count = new_peer_set.peers.len();\n        if peer_count == 0 {\n            warn!(\"peer discovery returned empty peer set\");\n            return Err(ModeAError::NoPeers);\n        }\n\n        // Update cached peer set\n        let mut cached = self.cached_peer_set.write().await;\n        *cached = new_peer_set;\n\n        debug!(\n            pod_id = %self.pod_id,\n            peer_count,\n            \"refreshed Mode A peer set\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(peer_count)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current peer set.\n    pub async fn peer_set(&self) -> PeerSet {\n        self.cached_peer_set.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Compute the rendezvous score for a shard-peer pair.\n    ///\n    /// Higher score = higher ownership priority.\n    /// Uses xxhash (twox-hash) for consistency with router.\n    fn rendezvous_score(shard_id: &str, peer_id: &str) -> u64 {\n        let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n        hasher.write(shard_id.as_bytes());\n        hasher.write(b\"||\");\n        hasher.write(peer_id.as_bytes());\n        hasher.finish()\n    }\n\n    /// Find the peer that owns a given shard via rendezvous hashing.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the peer ID with the highest rendezvous score for the shard.\n    pub async fn owner_for_shard(&self, shard_id: &str) -> Result<PeerId> {\n        if shard_id.is_empty() {\n            return Err(ModeAError::EmptyShardId);\n        }\n\n        let peer_set = self.peer_set().await;\n\n        if peer_set.peers.is_empty() {\n            return Err(ModeAError::NoPeers);\n        }\n\n        let mut best_peer = None;\n        let mut best_score = 0u64;\n\n        for peer in &peer_set.peers {\n            let score = Self::rendezvous_score(shard_id, peer);\n            if score > best_score {\n                best_score = score;\n                best_peer = Some(peer.clone());\n            }\n        }\n\n        best_peer.ok_or(ModeAError::NoPeers)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this pod owns a given shard.\n    ///\n    /// Returns true if this pod has the highest rendezvous score for the shard.\n    pub async fn owns_shard(&self, shard_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        if shard_id.is_empty() {\n            return Err(ModeAError::EmptyShardId);\n        }\n\n        let owner = self.owner_for_shard(shard_id).await?;\n        Ok(owner == self.pod_id)\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this pod owns a task (by miroir_id).\n    ///\n    /// Uses the same rendezvous hashing as shard ownership.\n    /// Task registry pruner uses this to partition task cleanup.\n    pub async fn owns_task(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        if miroir_id.is_empty() {\n            return Err(ModeAError::EmptyShardId);\n        }\n\n        let peer_set = self.peer_set().await;\n\n        if peer_set.peers.is_empty() {\n            return Err(ModeAError::NoPeers);\n        }\n\n        let mut best_score = 0u64;\n        let mut is_owner = false;\n\n        for peer in &peer_set.peers {\n            let score = Self::rendezvous_score(miroir_id, peer);\n            if score > best_score {\n                best_score = score;\n                is_owner = peer == &self.pod_id;\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(is_owner)\n    }\n\n    /// Synchronous version of `owns_task` for use with sync callbacks.\n    ///\n    /// Uses `try_read` on the cached peer set to avoid blocking.\n    /// Returns `true` if ownership can be determined and this pod owns the task,\n    /// `false` if ownership can be determined and this pod doesn't own it,\n    /// or `false` if the peer set lock is contended (safe default: skip).\n    ///\n    /// This is designed for use with the task pruner's `mode_a_owner_fn` callback.\n    pub fn owns_task_sync(&self, miroir_id: &str) -> bool {\n        if miroir_id.is_empty() {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        // Try to read the cached peer set without blocking\n        let peer_set = match self.cached_peer_set.try_read() {\n            Ok(guard) => guard.clone(),\n            Err(_) => {\n                // Lock is contended, return false (safe default: skip this task)\n                // Another pod will handle it\n                return false;\n            }\n        };\n\n        if peer_set.peers.is_empty() {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        let mut best_score = 0u64;\n        let mut is_owner = false;\n\n        for peer in &peer_set.peers {\n            let score = Self::rendezvous_score(miroir_id, peer);\n            if score > best_score {\n                best_score = score;\n                is_owner = peer == &self.pod_id;\n            }\n        }\n\n        is_owner\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this pod owns a canary (by canary ID).\n    ///\n    /// Canary runner uses this to partition canary execution.\n    pub async fn owns_canary(&self, canary_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        self.owns_task(canary_id).await\n    }\n\n    /// Check if this pod owns an (index, node) pair for settings drift checking.\n    ///\n    /// Combines index and node into a single key for rendezvous hashing.\n    pub async fn owns_settings_check(&self, index_uid: &str, node_id: &str) -> Result<bool> {\n        let key = format!(\"{index_uid}:{node_id}\");\n        self.owns_task(&key).await\n    }\n\n    /// Get the list of shards owned by this pod.\n    ///\n    /// Computes ownership for all shards and returns the ones this pod owns.\n    pub async fn owned_shards(&self, all_shards: &[u32]) -> Result<Vec<u32>> {\n        let peer_set = self.peer_set().await;\n\n        if peer_set.peers.is_empty() {\n            return Err(ModeAError::NoPeers);\n        }\n\n        let mut owned = Vec::new();\n\n        for &shard_id in all_shards {\n            let shard_str = shard_id.to_string();\n            if self.owns_shard(&shard_str).await? {\n                owned.push(shard_id);\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(owned)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the fraction of shards owned by this pod.\n    ///\n    /// Returns a value between 0.0 and 1.0.\n    pub async fn ownership_fraction(&self, all_shards: &[u32]) -> Result<f64> {\n        let owned = self.owned_shards(all_shards).await?;\n        let total = all_shards.len() as f64;\n        let owned_count = owned.len() as f64;\n\n        if total > 0.0 {\n            Ok(owned_count / total)\n        } else {\n            Ok(0.0)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current peer count.\n    pub async fn peer_count(&self) -> usize {\n        self.peer_set().await.peers.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we are the only peer (single-pod deployment).\n    pub async fn is_single_pod(&self) -> bool {\n        self.peer_count().await <= 1\n    }\n\n    /// Get our pod ID.\n    pub fn pod_id(&self) -> &str {\n        &self.pod_id\n    }\n\n    /// Set the peer set directly (test-only).\n    ///\n    /// This method is only intended for use in tests to simulate different\n    /// peer configurations without going through peer discovery. It is reachable\n    /// from in-crate unit tests (`cfg(test)`) and from integration tests / other\n    /// crates that enable the `test-helpers` feature — integration tests link\n    /// against the non-test build of the library, so `cfg(test)` alone would\n    /// strip it.\n    #[cfg(any(test, feature = \"test-helpers\"))]\n    pub async fn set_peer_set_for_test(&self, peer_set: PeerSet) {\n        let mut cached = self.cached_peer_set.write().await;\n        *cached = peer_set;\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rendezvous_score_deterministic() {\n        // Same inputs should produce same score\n        let score1 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-42\", \"pod-1\");\n        let score2 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-42\", \"pod-1\");\n        assert_eq!(score1, score2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rendezvous_score_different_peers() {\n        // Different peers should produce different scores\n        let score1 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-42\", \"pod-1\");\n        let score2 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-42\", \"pod-2\");\n        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_rendezvous_score_different_shards() {\n        // Different shards should produce different scores\n        let score1 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-1\", \"pod-1\");\n        let score2 = ModeACoordinator::rendezvous_score(\"shard-2\", \"pod-1\");\n        assert_ne!(score1, score2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_owns_shard_empty_id() {\n        let coordinator = test_coordinator();\n        tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap().block_on(async {\n            let result = coordinator.owns_shard(\"\").await;\n            assert!(matches!(result, Err(ModeAError::EmptyShardId)));\n        });\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_owns_task_by_miroir_id() {\n        let coordinator = test_coordinator();\n        tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap().block_on(async {\n            // With single pod, we own everything\n            let result = coordinator.owns_task(\"miroir-task-123\").await;\n            assert!(result.unwrap());\n        });\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_owns_settings_check() {\n        let coordinator = test_coordinator();\n        tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap().block_on(async {\n            // With single pod, we own everything\n            let result = coordinator.owns_settings_check(\"my-index\", \"node-1\").await;\n            assert!(result.unwrap());\n        });\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_owned_shards() {\n        let coordinator = test_coordinator();\n        tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap().block_on(async {\n            let all_shards: Vec<u32> = (0..10).collect();\n            let owned = coordinator.owned_shards(&all_shards).await.unwrap();\n\n            // With single pod, we own all shards\n            assert_eq!(owned.len(), 10);\n        });\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_ownership_fraction() {\n        let coordinator = test_coordinator();\n        tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap().block_on(async {\n            let all_shards: Vec<u32> = (0..10).collect();\n            let fraction = coordinator.ownership_fraction(&all_shards).await.unwrap();\n\n            // With single pod, we own 100% of shards\n            assert_eq!(fraction, 1.0);\n        });\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_is_single_pod() {\n        let coordinator = test_coordinator();\n        tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap().block_on(async {\n            assert!(coordinator.is_single_pod().await);\n        });\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_no_peers_error() {\n        // Create a coordinator with an empty peer set\n        let peer_discovery = Arc::new(PeerDiscovery::new(\n            \"test-pod\".to_string(),\n            \"default\".to_string(),\n            \"miroir-headless\".to_string(),\n        ));\n\n        let coordinator = ModeACoordinator::new(\"test-pod\".to_string(), peer_discovery);\n\n        // Manually set empty peer set\n        let empty_set = PeerSet::new(vec![]);\n        *coordinator.cached_peer_set.write().await = empty_set;\n\n        let result = coordinator.owns_shard(\"shard-1\").await;\n        assert!(matches!(result, Err(ModeAError::NoPeers)));\n    }\n\n    /// Acceptance test (P6.3): owns() returns true for exactly one peer per item across the peer set.\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_owns_exactly_one_peer_per_item() {\n        // Create multiple coordinators representing different pods\n        let peer_discovery_1 = Arc::new(PeerDiscovery::new(\n            \"pod-1\".to_string(),\n            \"default\".to_string(),\n            \"miroir-headless\".to_string(),\n        ));\n        let coordinator_1 = ModeACoordinator::new(\"pod-1\".to_string(), peer_discovery_1);\n\n        let peer_discovery_2 = Arc::new(PeerDiscovery::new(\n            \"pod-2\".to_string(),\n            \"default\".to_string(),\n            \"miroir-headless\".to_string(),\n        ));\n        let coordinator_2 = ModeACoordinator::new(\"pod-2\".to_string(), peer_discovery_2);\n\n        let peer_discovery_3 = Arc::new(PeerDiscovery::new(\n            \"pod-3\".to_string(),\n            \"default\".to_string(),\n            \"miroir-headless\".to_string(),\n        ));\n        let coordinator_3 = ModeACoordinator::new(\"pod-3\".to_string(), peer_discovery_3);\n\n        // Set up a shared peer set with all 3 pods\n        let peer_set = PeerSet::new(vec![\n            \"pod-1\".to_string(),\n            \"pod-2\".to_string(),\n            \"pod-3\".to_string(),\n        ]);\n        *coordinator_1.cached_peer_set.write().await = peer_set.clone();\n        *coordinator_2.cached_peer_set.write().await = peer_set.clone();\n        *coordinator_3.cached_peer_set.write().await = Shared per-node index-stats aggregation.\n//!\n//! Extracted from `ilm::fetch_index_stats` (plan §13.17) so that both ILM\n//! rollover evaluation and the reshard backfill denominator (bf-2ynu5) compute\n//! an index's real document count the *same* way: iterate the source node\n//! addresses, `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats`, and reduce `numberOfDocuments`.\n//!\n//! # Why `max`, not `sum`\n//!\n//! Each address in a coordinator's `node_addresses` hosts a full replica of the\n//! index (this is also why `reshard::advance_backfill` can read every shard of\n//! the source index from a single healthy node via `filter=_miroir_shard`).\n//! Reducing per-node counts with `max` therefore yields the true document count\n//! and tolerates a replica that is mid-ingest; `sum` would over-count by the\n//! replication factor. ILM relies on exactly this reduction for its `max_docs`\n//! rollover trigger, so the reshard denominator must agree with it.\n//!\n//! # Testability\n//!\n//! The HTTP transport ([`fetch_node_stats`]) is exercised end-to-end by the\n//! `miroir-proxy` ILM acceptance tests (mockito). The reduction policy itself is\n//! a pure function — [`reduce_document_counts`] — so it is unit-tested directly\n//! against known per-node counts (including failing nodes) without spinning up\n//! an HTTP server.\n\nuse reqwest::Client;\nuse serde::Deserialize;\nuse tracing::warn;\n\n/// `/indexes/{uid}/stats` response shape for a single node (Meilisearch).\n///\n/// Both fields default when absent so a minimal `{\"numberOfDocuments\": N}` body\n/// parses cleanly, and a node that lacks the index entirely yields zeros.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\n#[allow(non_snake_case)]\npub struct NodeIndexStats {\n    #[serde(default)]\n    #[serde(rename = \"numberOfDocuments\")]\n    pub number_of_documents: u64,\n    /// Size detail — may be absent on older Meilisearch versions.\n    #[serde(rename = \"stats\", default)]\n    pub stats: Option<NodeStatsDetail>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize, Default)]\npub struct NodeStatsDetail {\n    #[serde(rename = \"databaseSize\", default)]\n    pub database_size: u64,\n}\n\n/// Aggregated index stats reduced across all source node addresses.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\npub struct IndexStats {\n    /// Document count, reduced with `max` across responding nodes.\n    pub total_documents: u64,\n    /// `databaseSize` summed across responding nodes.\n    pub total_size_bytes: u64,\n    /// Number of nodes that returned stats successfully (HTTP 2xx, parsable).\n    pub nodes_responded: usize,\n}\n\n/// Failure encountered while fetching stats from one node.\n#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum FetchStatsError {\n    #[error(\"request failed: {0}\")]\n    Request(#[from] reqwest::Error),\n    #[error(\"failed to read response: {0}\")]\n    Read(reqwest::Error),\n    #[error(\"HTTP {status}: {body}\")]\n    Http { status: u16, body: String },\n    #[error(\"failed to parse stats: {0}\")]\n    Parse(serde_json::Error),\n}\n\n/// Fetch `/indexes/{uid}/stats` from a single node.\n///\n/// A 404 (index absent on that node) is reported as zero documents rather than\n/// an error, matching the original `ilm::fetch_node_stats` behavior — a node\n/// that has not yet received the index should not fail the aggregation.\npub async fn fetch_node_stats(\n    client: &Client,\n    address: &str,\n    master_key: &str,\n    index_uid: &str,\n) -> Result<NodeIndexStats, FetchStatsError> {\n    let url = format!(\n        \"{}/indexes/{}/stats\",\n        address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n        index_uid\n    );\n\n    let response = client\n        .get(&url)\n        .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", master_key))\n        .send()\n        .await\n        .map_err(FetchStatsError::Request)?;\n\n    let status = response.status();\n    let body_text = response\n        .text()\n        .await\n        .map_err(FetchStatsError::Read)?;\n\n    if status.as_u16() == 404 {\n        // Index doesn't exist on this node — count as zero, not an error.\n        return Ok(NodeIndexStats {\n            number_of_documents: 0,\n            stats: None,\n        });\n    }\n\n    if !status.is_success() {\n        return Err(FetchStatsError::Http {\n            status: status.as_u16(),\n            body: body_text,\n        });\n    }\n\n    serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(FetchStatsError::Parse)\n}\n\n/// Aggregate an index's stats across all source node addresses.\n///\n/// Iterates every address, GETs `/indexes/{uid}/stats`, and reduces the results\n/// the same way `ilm::fetch_index_stats` did: document count via `max`, size via\n/// `sum`. A node that errors (network / non-2xx / parse) is logged and skipped\n/// — one failing node never blocks the count. Returns zeros only if every node\n/// fails or reports zero.\npub async fn aggregate_index_stats(\n    client: &Client,\n    node_addresses: &[String],\n    master_key: &str,\n    index_uid: &str,\n) -> IndexStats {\n    let mut total_documents = 0u64;\n    let mut total_size_bytes = 0u64;\n    let mut nodes_responded = 0usize;\n\n    for address in node_addresses {\n        match fetch_node_stats(client, address, master_key, index_uid).await {\n            Ok(node_stats) => {\n                total_documents = total_documents.max(node_stats.number_of_documents);\n                if let Some(ref stats) = node_stats.stats {\n                    total_size_bytes += stats.database_size;\n                }\n                nodes_responded += 1;\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                // Log but continue - one node failing shouldn't block the count.\n                warn!(\"index_stats: failed to fetch stats from node {}: {}\", address, e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    IndexStats {\n        total_documents,\n        total_size_bytes,\n        nodes_responded,\n    }\n}\n\n/// Reduce per-node document counts into the index's real document count.\n///\n/// Pure (no I/O): takes each node's count as `Ok(n)` (or `Err(_)` if that node\n/// failed to respond) and returns the `max` across the responders, or `0` when\n/// no node responded. This is the policy shared by ILM rollover evaluation and\n/// the reshard backfill denominator, factored out so it can be unit-tested\n/// against known per-node counts without an HTTP server.\npub fn reduce_document_counts<I, E>(counts: I) -> u64\nwhere\n    I: IntoIterator<Item = Result<u64, E>>,\n{\n    counts\n        .into_iter()\n        .filter_map(Result::ok)\n        .max()\n        .unwrap_or(0)\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn parse_node_stats_full_body() {\n        let body = r#\"{\"numberOfDocuments\": 4242, \"stats\": {\"databaseSize\": 9999}}\"#;\n        let stats: NodeIndexStats = serde_json::from_str(body).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(stats.number_of_documents, 4242);\n        let detail = stats.stats.expect(\"stats detail present\");\n        assert_eq!(detail.database_size, 9999);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn parse_node_stats_missing_fields_default_zero() {\n        // A bare object must still parse, defaulting every field.\n        let stats: NodeIndexStats = serde_json::from_str(\"{}\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(stats.number_of_documents, 0);\n        assert!(stats.stats.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn parse_node_stats_docs_without_size_detail() {\n        let stats: NodeIndexStats = serde_json::from_str(r#\"{\"numberOfDocuments\": 7}\"#).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(stats.number_of_documents, 7);\n        assert!(stats.stats.is_none());\n    }\n\n    // --- reduce_document_counts: the \"mock node client\" aggregation seam ---\n    // Each `Ok(n)` is a node that returned `numberOfDocuments = n`; each `Err`\n    // is a node that failed to respond. The reduction must pick the maximum\n    // responder and tolerate any number of failures.\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_picks_max_across_healthy_nodes() {\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 3] = [Ok(100), Ok(250), Ok(180)];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 250);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_ignores_failed_nodes() {\n        // Two of four nodes failed; the denominator is still the max responder.\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 4] = [Ok(100), Err(()), Ok(250), Err(())];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 250);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_zero_when_every_node_fails() {\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 3] = [Err(()), Err(()), Err(())];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_zero_when_no_nodes() {\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 0] = [];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_single_node() {\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 1] = [Ok(42)];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 42);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn reduce_treats_zero_doc_index_as_zero() {\n        // An existing but empty index reports 0 on every node.\n        let counts: [Result<u64, ()>; 2] = [Ok(0), Ok(0)];\n        assert_eq!(reduce_document_counts(counts), 0);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":252,"startLine":1,"totalLines":252}}}

Six-phase resharding process:\n//! 1. Shadow create\n//! 2. Dual-hash dual-write\n//! 3. Backfill\n//! 4. Verify\n//! 5. Alias swap\n//! 6. Cleanup\n\nuse crate::anti_entropy::{AntiEntropyConfig, AntiEntropyReconciler};\nuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse crate::scatter::{FetchDocumentsRequest, NodeClient, WriteRequest};\nuse crate::task_store::TaskStore;\nuse crate::topology::Topology;\nuse reqwest::Client;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::hash::Hasher;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\nuse tracing::{debug, info, warn};\nuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\nuse uuid::Uuid;\n\n/// Resharding operation state persisted in task store.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ReshardState {\n    pub id: Uuid,\n    pub index_uid: String,\n    pub old_shards: u32,\n    pub new_shards: u32,\n    pub phase: Phase,\n    pub shadow_index: Option<String>,\n    pub started_at: u64,\n    pub updated_at: u64,\n    pub backfill_progress: BackfillProgress,\n    pub verify_result: Option<VerifyResult>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct BackfillProgress {\n    pub total_documents: u64,\n    pub processed_documents: u64,\n    pub current_shard: Option<u32>,\n    pub last_cursor: Option<String>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct VerifyResult {\n    pub passed: bool,\n    pub mismatches: Vec<MismatchDetail>,\n    pub fingerprint_live: String,\n    pub fingerprint_shadow: String,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct MismatchDetail {\n    pub primary_key: String,\n    pub shard_old: u32,\n    pub shard_new: u32,\n    pub hash_live: Option<String>,\n    pub hash_shadow: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Resharding executor - handles the six-phase process.\npub struct ReshardExecutor<C: NodeClient> {\n    state: Arc<RwLock<ReshardState>>,\n    topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n    config: ReshardConfig,\n    node_client: Arc<C>,\n    task_store: Option<Arc<dyn TaskStore>>,\n    /// HTTP client for node operations (index creation, settings copy).\n    http_client: Arc<Client>,\n    /// Node addresses for HTTP operations.\n    node_addresses: Arc<Vec<String>>,\n    /// Master key for node authentication.\n    master_key: Arc<String>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct ReshardConfig {\n    pub backfill_concurrency: usize,\n    pub backfill_batch_size: usize,\n    pub throttle_docs_per_sec: u64,\n    pub verify_before_swap: bool,\n    pub retain_old_index_hours: u64,\n}\n\nimpl<C: NodeClient> ReshardExecutor<C> {\n    /// Create a new resharding operation.\n    pub fn new(\n        index_uid: String,\n        old_shards: u32,\n        new_shards: u32,\n        topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n        config: ReshardConfig,\n        node_client: Arc<C>,\n        task_store: Option<Arc<dyn TaskStore>>,\n        http_client: Arc<Client>,\n        node_addresses: Arc<Vec<String>>,\n        master_key: Arc<String>,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let id = Uuid::new_v4();\n        let now = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap()\n            .as_secs();\n\n        let state = ReshardState {\n            id,\n            index_uid,\n            old_shards,\n            new_shards,\n            phase: Phase::Idle,\n            shadow_index: None,\n            started_at: now,\n            updated_at: now,\n            backfill_progress: BackfillProgress {\n                total_documents: 0,\n                processed_documents: 0,\n                current_shard: None,\n                last_cursor: None,\n            },\n            verify_result: None,\n        };\n\n        Self {\n            state: Arc::new(RwLock::new(state)),\n            topology,\n            config,\n            node_client,\n            task_store,\n            http_client,\n            node_addresses,\n            master_key,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current state.\n    pub async fn state(&self) -> ReshardState {\n        self.state.read().await.clone()\n    }\n\n    /// Start or advance the resharding operation.\n    pub async fn advance(&self) -> Result<Phase> {\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n        let current_phase = state.phase;\n\n        let next_phase = match current_phase {\n            Phase::Idle => {\n                // Phase 1: Create shadow index\n                self.create_shadow_index(&mut state).await?;\n                Phase::Shadow\n            }\n            Phase::Shadow => {\n                // Phase 2: Start dual-write mode\n                self.start_dual_write(&mut state).await?;\n                Phase::DualWrite\n            }\n            Phase::DualWrite => {\n                // Phase 3: Start backfill\n                self.start_backfill(&mut state).await?;\n                Phase::Backfill\n            }\n            Phase::Backfill => {\n                // Check if backfill complete\n                if self.is_backfill_complete(&state).await? {\n                    // Phase 4: Verify\n                    if self.config.verify_before_swap {\n                        self.run_verify(&mut state).await?;\n                        Phase::Verify\n                    } else {\n                        // Skip verify, go straight to swap\n                        Phase::Swap\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    // Continue backfill\n                    self.advance_backfill(&mut state).await?;\n                    Phase::Backfill\n                }\n            }\n            Phase::Verify => {\n                let verify_passed = state\n                    .verify_result\n                    .as_ref()\n                    .map(|v| v.passed)\n                    .unwrap_or(false);\n\n                if !verify_passed {\n                    return Err(MiroirError::VerificationFailed(\n                        \"Resharding verification failed\".to_string(),\n                    ));\n                }\n\n                // Phase 5: Alias swap\n                self.alias_swap(&mut state).await?;\n                Phase::Swap\n            }\n            Phase::Swap => {\n                // Phase 6: Cleanup (scheduled for later)\n                Phase::Cleanup\n            }\n            Phase::Cleanup => {\n                // Operation complete\n                Phase::Complete\n            }\n            Phase::Complete => {\n                return Ok(Phase::Complete);\n            }\n        };\n\n        state.phase = next_phase;\n        state.updated_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap()\n            .as_secs();\n\n        Ok(next_phase)\n    }\n\n    /// Phase 1: Create shadow index on all nodes.\n    ///\n    /// Implements plan §13.1 step 1: Create index `{uid}__reshard_{S_new}` on every node\n    /// with the new shard count, propagating the live index's settings via the two-phase\n    /// broadcast (§13.5).\n    async fn create_shadow_index(&self, state: &mut ReshardState) -> Result<()> {\n        let shadow_name = format!(\"{}__reshard_{}\", state.index_uid, state.new_shards);\n        state.shadow_index = Some(shadow_name.clone());\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            index = %state.index_uid,\n            shadow = %shadow_name,\n            old_shards = state.old_shards,\n            new_shards = state.new_shards,\n            \"Phase 1: Creating shadow index on all nodes\"\n        );\n\n        // Step 1: Get the primary key from the live index (first node)\n        let primary_key = self.get_index_primary_key(&state.index_uid).await?;\n\n        // Step 2: Create the shadow index on all nodes\n        self.create_index_on_all_nodes(&shadow_name, &primary_key)\n            .await?;\n\n        // Step 3: Copy settings from live index to shadow index\n        // For now, we use a simple sequential approach. Two-phase broadcast (§13.5)\n        // would be used for production-grade settings propagation.\n        self.copy_index_settings(&state.index_uid, &shadow_name)\n            .await?;\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            index = %state.index_uid,\n            shadow = %shadow_name,\n            \"Shadow index created and settings propagated\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Get the primary key of an index by querying the first node.\n    async fn get_index_primary_key(&self, index_uid: &str) -> Result<String> {\n        let address = self\n            .node_addresses\n            .first()\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Topology(\"No node addresses available\".to_string()))?;\n\n        let url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), index_uid);\n\n        let response = self\n            .http_client\n            .get(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", &*self.master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| {\n                MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"failed to get index info from {address}: {e}\"))\n            })?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        let body_text = response\n            .text()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"failed to read response: {e}\")))?;\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                \"failed to get index '{index_uid}' on node {address}: HTTP {}: {body_text}\",\n                status.as_u16()\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let index_info: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n            MiroirError::InvalidState(format!(\"failed to parse index info JSON: {e}\"))\n        })?;\n\n        let primary_key = index_info\n            .get(\"primaryKey\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| {\n                MiroirError::InvalidState(format!(\"index '{index_uid}' has no primaryKey field\"))\n            })?;\n\n        Ok(primary_key.to_string())\n    }\n\n    /// Create an index on all nodes with the given primary key.\n    async fn create_index_on_all_nodes(&self, index_uid: &str, primary_key: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut created_on: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n        for address in self.node_addresses.iter() {\n            let url = format!(\"{}/indexes\", address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n\n            let body = serde_json::json!({\n                \"uid\": index_uid,\n                \"primaryKey\": primary_key,\n            });\n\n            let response = self\n                .http_client\n                .post(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", &*self.master_key))\n                .json(&body)\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| {\n                    MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"request to {address} failed: {e}\"))\n                })?;\n\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body_text = response.text().await.map_err(|e| {\n                MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"failed to read response: {e}\"))\n            })?;\n\n            if status.as_u16() == 409 {\n                // Index already exists - this is ok for resharding (might have been partially created)\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    index = %index_uid,\n                    node = %address,\n                    \"Index already exists on node\"\n                );\n                created_on.push(address.clone());\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            if !status.is_success() {\n                // Rollback: delete index on all previously created nodes\n                self.rollback_delete_index(index_uid, &created_on).await;\n\n                return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                    \"failed to create index '{index_uid}' on node {address}: HTTP {}: {body_text}\",\n                    status.as_u16()\n                )));\n            }\n\n            created_on.push(address.clone());\n            tracing::debug!(\n                index = %index_uid,\n                node = %address,\n                \"Created index on node\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            index = %index_uid,\n            nodes = created_on.len(),\n            \"Index created on all nodes\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Copy settings from the source index to the target index.\n    ///\n    /// This reads settings from the source index (first node) and applies them\n    /// to the target index on all nodes. Uses a simple sequential approach;\n    /// two-phase broadcast (§13.5) would be used for production-grade propagation.\n    async fn copy_index_settings(&self, source_index: &str, target_index: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        let address = self\n            .node_addresses\n            .first()\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Topology(\"No node addresses available\".to_string()))?;\n\n        // Get settings from the source index\n        let url = format!(\n            \"{}/indexes/{}/settings\",\n            address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n            source_index\n        );\n\n        let response = self\n            .http_client\n            .get(&url)\n            .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", &*self.master_key))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| {\n                MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"failed to get settings from {address}: {e}\"))\n            })?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        let body_text = response\n            .text()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"failed to read response: {e}\")))?;\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                \"failed to get settings for index '{source_index}' on node {address}: HTTP {}: {body_text}\",\n                status.as_u16()\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let settings: Value = serde_json::from_str(&body_text).map_err(|e| {\n            MiroirError::InvalidState(format!(\"failed to parse settings JSON: {e}\"))\n        })?;\n\n        // Apply settings to the target index on all nodes\n        for address in self.node_addresses.iter() {\n            let url = format!(\n                \"{}/indexes/{}/settings\",\n                address.trim_end_matches('/'),\n                target_index\n            );\n\n            let response = self\n                .http_client\n                .patch(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", &*self.master_key))\n                .json(&settings)\n                .send()\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| {\n                    MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                        \"failed to patch settings on {address}: {e}\"\n                    ))\n                })?;\n\n            let status = response.status();\n            let body_text = response.text().await.map_err(|e| {\n                MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"failed to read response: {e}\"))\n            })?;\n\n            if !status.is_success() {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    source_index = %source_index,\n                    target_index = %target_index,\n                    node = %address,\n                    status = status.as_u16(),\n                    body = %body_text,\n                    \"Failed to copy settings to node\"\n                );\n            } else {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    target_index = %target_index,\n                    node = %address,\n                    \"Settings copied to node\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            source_index = %source_index,\n            target_index = %target_index,\n            \"Settings copied from source to target index\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Rollback: delete index on all specified nodes.\n    async fn rollback_delete_index(&self, index_uid: &str, nodes: &[String]) {\n        for address in nodes {\n            let url = format!(\"{}/indexes/{}\", address.trim_end_matches('/'), index_uid);\n\n            match self\n                .http_client\n                .delete(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", &*self.master_key))\n                .send()\n                .await\n            {\n                Ok(_) => {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        index = %index_uid,\n                        node = %address,\n                        \"Rollback: deleted index on node\"\n                    )\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::error!(\n                        index = %index_uid,\n                        node = %address,\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"Rollback: failed to delete index on node\"\n                    )\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Phase 2: Start dual-write mode.\n    async fn start_dual_write(&self, state: &mut ReshardState) -> Result<()> {\n        tracing::info!(\n            index = %state.index_uid,\n            \"Started dual-write mode (old + new shard assignments)\"\n        );\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Phase 3: Start backfill from live to shadow.\n    async fn start_backfill(&self, state: &mut ReshardState) -> Result<()> {\n        // Get total document count for progress tracking\n        // TODO: Query nodes for document counts\n\n        state.backfill_progress = BackfillProgress {\n            total_documents: 0, // Will be updated\n            processed_documents: 0,\n            current_shard: Some(0),\n            last_cursor: None,\n        };\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            index = %state.index_uid,\n            \"Started backfill\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Check if backfill is complete.\n    async fn is_backfill_complete(&self, state: &ReshardState) -> Result<bool> {\n        Ok(state\n            .backfill_progress\n            .current_shard\n            .map(|s| s >= state.old_shards)\n            .unwrap_or(false))\n    }\n\n    /// Advance backfill by processing one shard.\n    ///\n    /// Implements plan §13.1 step 3: background streamer pages every live-index shard\n    /// using `filter=_miroir_shard={id}`. Each document is re-hashed under `S_new`\n    /// and written to the shadow. Documents are tagged with `_miroir_origin: reshard_backfill`\n    /// for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n    async fn advance_backfill(&self, state: &mut ReshardState) -> Result<()> {\n        let shard_id = state.backfill_progress.current_shard.unwrap_or(0);\n\n        debug!(\n            index = %state.index_uid,\n            shard = shard_id,\n            \"Starting backfill for shard\"\n        );\n\n        // Get a healthy node from topology\n        let topology = self.topology.read().await;\n        let node = topology.nodes().find(|n| n.is_healthy()).ok_or_else(|| {\n            MiroirError::Topology(\"No healthy nodes available for backfill\".to_string())\n        })?;\n        let node_id = node.id.clone();\n        let node_address = node.address.clone();\n        drop(topology);\n\n        // Get primary key field name\n        let primary_key = self.get_index_primary_key(&state.index_uid).await?;\n\n        // Get shadow index name\n        let shadow_name = state\n            .shadow_index\n            .as_ref()\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"Shadow index not created\".to_string()))?\n            .clone();\n\n        // Paginated fetch from live index with filter=_miroir_shard={shard_id}\n        let mut offset = 0u32;\n        let limit = self.config.backfill_batch_size as u32;\n        let mut total_processed = 0u64;\n        let mut total_docs_in_shard = 0u64;\n\n        loop {\n            let fetch_request = FetchDocumentsRequest {\n                index_uid: state.index_uid.clone(),\n                filter: json!({ \"_miroir_shard\": shard_id }),\n                limit,\n                offset,\n            };\n\n            let fetch_response = self\n                .node_client\n                .fetch_documents(&node_id, &node_address, &fetch_request)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| match e {\n                    crate::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { status, body } => {\n                        MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                            \"fetch from node {node_id} failed: HTTP {status} - {body}\"\n                        ))\n                    }\n                    crate::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(msg) => MiroirError::InvalidRequest(\n                        format!(\"fetch from node {node_id} failed: network error - {msg}\"),\n                    ),\n                    crate::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => {\n                        MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\"fetch from node {node_id} timed out\"))\n                    }\n                })?;\n\n            // Update total count on first page\n            if offset == 0 {\n                total_docs_in_shard = fetch_response.total;\n                state.backfill_progress.total_documents += total_docs_in_shard;\n            }\n\n            let docs = fetch_response.results;\n            let docs_in_page = docs.len() as u64;\n\n            if docs_in_page == 0 {\n                // No more documents in this shard\n                break;\n            }\n\n            // Process each document: re-hash and write to shadow\n            for mut doc in docs {\n                // Extract primary key value\n                let pk_value = doc\n                    .get(&primary_key)\n                    .and_then(|v| {\n                        if v.is_string() {\n                            v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string())\n                        } else {\n                            Some(v.to_string())\n                        }\n                    })\n                    .ok_or_else(|| {\n                        MiroirError::InvalidState(format!(\n                            \"document missing primary key field '{primary_key}'\"\n                        ))\n                    })?;\n\n                // Re-hash under new shard count\n                let new_shard_id = hash_pk_to_shard(&pk_value, state.new_shards);\n\n                // Inject _miroir_shard = new_shard_id\n                doc.as_object_mut()\n                    .unwrap()\n                    .insert(\"_miroir_shard\".to_string(), json!(new_shard_id));\n\n                // Write to shadow index with origin tag for CDC suppression\n                let write_request = WriteRequest {\n                    index_uid: shadow_name.clone(),\n                    documents: vec![doc],\n                    primary_key: Some(primary_key.clone()),\n                    origin: Some(\"reshard_backfill\".to_string()),\n                };\n\n                let write_response = self\n                    .node_client\n                    .write_documents(&node_id, &node_address, &write_request)\n                    .await\n                    .map_err(|e| match e {\n                        crate::scatter::NodeError::HttpError { status, body } => {\n                            MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                                \"write to node {node_id} failed: HTTP {status} - {body}\"\n                            ))\n                        }\n                        crate::scatter::NodeError::NetworkError(msg) => {\n                            MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                                \"write to node {node_id} failed: network error - {msg}\"\n                            ))\n                        }\n                        crate::scatter::NodeError::Timeout => MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                            \"write to node {node_id} timed out\"\n                        )),\n                    })?;\n\n                if !write_response.success {\n                    return Err(MiroirError::InvalidRequest(format!(\n                        \"write to shadow index failed: {}\",\n                        write_response\n                            .message\n                            .unwrap_or_else(|| \"unknown error\".to_string())\n                    )));\n                }\n\n                total_processed += 1;\n            }\n\n            state.backfill_progress.processed_documents += docs_in_page;\n\n            // Move to next page\n            offset += limit;\n\n            // Apply throttling after each batch\n            if self.config.throttle_docs_per_sec > 0 {\n                let docs_per_ms = self.config.throttle_docs_per_sec as f64 / 1000.0;\n                let target_delay_ms = (docs_in_page as f64 / docs_per_ms).ceil() as u64;\n                if target_delay_ms > 0 {\n                    tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(target_delay_ms)).await;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Check if we've processed all documents in this shard\n            if offset as u64 >= total_docs_in_shard {\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n\n        debug!(\n            index = %state.index_uid,\n            shard = shard_id,\n            processed = total_processed,\n            \"Completed backfill for shard\"\n        );\n\n        // Move to next shard\n        state.backfill_progress.current_shard = Some(shard_id + 1);\n        state.backfill_progress.last_cursor = Some(format!(\"shard_{shard_id}\"));\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Phase 4: Verify shadow index matches live index.\n    ///\n    /// Implements plan §13.1 step 4: cross-index PK-set comparator.\n    /// Iterates every shard of live + shadow via filter=_miroir_shard={id},\n    /// streams PKs + content fingerprints into side-by-side xxh3-keyed buckets,\n    /// and asserts: (a) live PK set == shadow PK set, (b) for each PK,\n    /// content_hash matches.\n    ///\n    /// Reuses §13.8's bucketed-Merkle machinery with PK-keyed (not shard-keyed)\n    /// bucketing so live and shadow can be compared across different S values.\n    async fn run_verify(&self, state: &mut ReshardState) -> Result<()> {\n        info!(\n            index = %state.index_uid,\n            old_shards = state.old_shards,\n            new_shards = state.new_shards,\n            \"Running cross-index verification (plan §13.1 step 4)\"\n        );\n\n        let shadow_name = state\n            .shadow_index\n            .as_ref()\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"Shadow index not created\".to_string()))?;\n\n        // Get a healthy node from topology for verification\n        let topology = self.topology.read().await;\n        let node = topology.nodes().find(|n| n.is_healthy()).ok_or_else(|| {\n            MiroirError::Topology(\"No healthy nodes available for verification\".to_string())\n        })?;\n        let node_id = node.id.clone();\n        let address = node.address.clone();\n        drop(topology);\n\n        // Perform cross-index comparison using PK-keyed bucketing\n        let config = AntiEntropyConfig {\n            index_uid: state.index_uid.clone(),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n        let reconciler =\n            AntiEntropyReconciler::new(config, self.topology.clone(), self.node_client.clone());\n\n        let diff = reconciler\n            .compare_index_buckets(\n                &node_id,\n                &address,\n                &state.index_uid,\n                state.old_shards,\n                &node_id,\n                &address,\n                shadow_name,\n                state.new_shards,\n            )\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| {\n                MiroirError::VerificationFailed(format!(\"Cross-index comparison failed: {e}\"))\n            })?;\n\n        // Build detailed mismatch list with shard assignments\n        let mut mismatch_details = Vec::new();\n        for pk in &diff.a_only_pks {\n            mismatch_details.push(MismatchDetail {\n                primary_key: pk.clone(),\n                shard_old: hash_pk_to_shard(pk, state.old_shards),\n                shard_new: hash_pk_to_shard(pk, state.new_shards),\n                hash_live: None,\n                hash_shadow: None,\n            });\n        }\n        for pk in &diff.b_only_pks {\n            mismatch_details.push(MismatchDetail {\n                primary_key: pk.clone(),\n                shard_old: hash_pk_to_shard(pk, state.old_shards),\n                shard_new: hash_pk_to_shard(pk, state.new_shards),\n                hash_live: None,\n                hash_shadow: None,\n            });\n        }\n        for pk in &diff.mismatched_pks {\n            mismatch_details.push(MismatchDetail {\n                primary_key: pk.clone(),\n                shard_old: hash_pk_to_shard(pk, state.old_shards),\n                shard_new: hash_pk_to_shard(pk, state.new_shards),\n                hash_live: None, // Could be populated with actual hashes if needed\n                hash_shadow: None,\n            });\n        }\n\n        let passed = mismatch_details.is_empty();\n\n        if !passed {\n            warn!(\n                index = %state.index_uid,\n                mismatches = mismatch_details.len(),\n                a_only = diff.a_only_pks.len(),\n                b_only = diff.b_only_pks.len(),\n                content_mismatch = diff.mismatched_pks.len(),\n                \"Verification failed: indexes differ\"\n            );\n        } else {\n            info!(\n                index = %state.index_uid,\n                \"Verification passed: indexes match\"\n            );\n        }\n\n        state.verify_result = Some(VerifyResult {\n            passed,\n            mismatches: mismatch_details,\n            fingerprint_live: format!(\"{}-shard\", state.old_shards),\n            fingerprint_shadow: format!(\"{}-shard\", state.new_shards),\n        });\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Phase 5: Atomic alias swap (P5.1.e, plan §13.1 step 5).\n    ///\n    /// Performs an atomic alias flip via the task store's flip_alias() method,\n    /// pointing the alias at the new shadow index. After this step, dual-write\n    /// stops and client writes target ONLY the new index.\n    async fn alias_swap(&self, state: &mut ReshardState) -> Result<()> {\n        let shadow_name = state\n            .shadow_index\n            .as_ref()\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::InvalidState(\"Shadow index not created\".to_string()))?;\n\n        let task_store = self.task_store.as_ref().ok_or_else(|| {\n            MiroirError::InvalidState(\"Task store required for alias swap\".to_string())\n        })?;\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            index = %state.index_uid,\n            shadow = %shadow_name,\n            \"Performing atomic alias swap (P5.1.e)\"\n        );\n\n        // Perform the atomic alias flip via task store\n        // This uses §13.7 atomic alias flip: PUT /_miroir/aliases/{index_uid} {\"target\": shadow_name}\n        let history_retention = 10; // Default history retention for rollback\n        let flipped = task_store\n            .flip_alias(&state.index_uid, shadow_name, history_retention)\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::AliasSwapFailed(format!(\"{e}\")))?;\n\n        if !flipped {\n            return Err(MiroirError::AliasSwapFailed(format!(\n                \"alias flip returned false for '{}' -> '{}'\",\n                state.index_uid, shadow_name\n            )));\n        }\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            index = %state.index_uid,\n            old_target = %state.index_uid,\n            new_target = %shadow_name,\n            \"alias swap completed: dual-write stopped\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Rollback the resharding operation (before phase 5).\n    pub async fn rollback(&self) -> Result<()> {\n        let mut state = self.state.write().await;\n\n        if state.phase >= Phase::Swap {\n            return Err(MiroirError::InvalidState(\n                \"Cannot rollback after alias swap\".to_string(),\n            ));\n        }\n\n        // Delete shadow index on all nodes\n        if let Some(ref shadow) = state.shadow_index {\n            tracing::info!(\n                index = %state.index_uid,\n                shadow = %shadow,\n                \"Rolling back: deleting shadow index on all nodes\"\n            );\n            self.rollback_delete_index(shadow, &self.node_addresses.to_vec())\n                .await;\n        }\n\n        state.phase = Phase::Complete;\n        state.updated_at = std::time::SystemTime::now()\n            .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap()\n            .as_secs();\n\n        tracing::info!(\n            index = %state.index_uid,\n            \"Resharding operation rolled back\"\n        );\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Hash a primary key to determine its shard assignment.\n///\n/// This matches the rendezvous hashing used for document routing.\n/// For verification purposes, we use a simple hash modulo since we're\n/// just showing which shard a PK would belong to.\npub fn hash_pk_to_shard(pk: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n    let mut hasher = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n    hasher.write(pk.as_bytes());\n    (hasher.finish() as u32) % shard_count\n}\n\n/// Phase of resharding operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub enum Phase {\n    Idle,\n    Shadow,\n    DualWrite,\n    Backfill,\n    Verify,\n    Swap,\n    Cleanup,\n    Complete,\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":529,"oldLines":6,"newStart":529,"newLines":36,"lines":["         Ok(())","     }"," ","+    /// It delegates to the shared [`crate::index_stats`] module so","+    /// that reshard and ILM rollover (plan §13.17) compute an index's document","+    /// count the *same* way: iterate the source `node_addresses`,","+    /// `GET /indexes/{uid}/stats`, and reduce `numberOfDocuments` with `max`","+    /// (each address hosts a full replica, so `sum` would over-count by the","+    /// replication factor).","+    ///","+    /// The aggregation is infallible from the caller's perspective — a node that","+    /// fails to respond (network / non-2xx / parse error) is logged and skipped,","+    /// and the count is `0` only if every node fails or reports zero — but this","+    /// returns [`Result`] to match the executor's method conventions and leave","+    /// the `start_backfill` caller (wired in a sibling bead) free to propagate a","+    /// future failure mode.","+    ///","+    /// Pure additive helper: this method is *not* called from the reshard state","+    /// machine in this bead.","+    pub async fn compute_source_document_count(&self, index_uid: &str) -> Result<u64> {","+        let stats = crate::index_stats::aggregate_index_stats(","+            &self.http_client,","+            &self.node_addresses,","+            &self.master_key,","+            index_uid,","+        )","+        .await;","+        Ok(stats.total_documents)","+    }","+","     /// Check if backfill is complete.","     async fn is_backfill_complete(&self, state: &ReshardState) -> Result<bool> {","         Ok(state"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
